purpose: Choose the primary data modeling approach before selecting vendor products.

branches.use_when: Many multi-entity invariants, joins, ad hoc filters, or reporting needs.
Aggregate documents are read/written together and internal shape changes often.
branches.recommend: Document model with clear aggregate boundaries.

branches.use_when: Relationship traversal is the core product behavior.
branches.recommend: Graph model or graph projection derived from canonical records.

branches.use_when: The product is fundamentally an append-only history of changes.
branches.recommend: Event model with projections for reads.

branches.use_when: Similarity search or retrieval is central but facts require governance.
branches.recommend: Hybrid canonical store plus vector/search index, not vector-only truth.

failure_modes: Choosing a document model despite cross-document invariants.
Using vector search as the source of truth.
Ignoring deletion and retention needs in event history.
Treating derived graph/search projections as canonical records without reconciliation.
output_checks: Describe migration path if the model changes.
